title: Method and apparatus for large scale manufacturing of nanoparticles
abstract: There is provided an apparatus for manufacturing nanoparticles comprising: a container comprising a liquid; an anode and a cathode arranged facing each other in the container, wherein the anode is movable in relation to the cathode; a power supply connected to the anode and the cathode; a current transducer control system comprising a current sensor for detecting a discharge current from an electrostatic discharge between the anode and the cathode, resulting in formation of metal nanoparticles through melting and evaporation of the anode. A motor controls the movement of the anode to control the distance between the anode and the cathode; and a control unit controls the motor to move the anode towards the cathode until a discharge current is detected by the current transducer control system, and when a discharge current is detected,to stop movement of the anode towards the cathode, and to determine a new movement speed and movement direction of the anode based on a consumption rate of the anode such that the discharge is interrupted.
claims: Claims 1 . An apparatus for manufacturing nanoparticles comprising: a container configured to contain a dielectric liquid; a cathode arranged in said container such that said cathode is covered by said liquid when said apparatus is in use, and an anode arranged facing said cathode, wherein said anode is movable in relation to said cathode such that a distance between said anode and said cathode is controllable; a power supply connected to said anode and said cathode to form an electric field therebetween; a current transducer control system comprising a current sensor arranged and configured to detect a discharge current from an electrostatic discharge between said anode and said cathode, wherein said discharge is configured to result in formation of metal nanoparticles through melting and evaporation of said anode, which nanoparticles are subsequently dispersed in said liquid; a motor configured to control said movement of said anode to control said distance between said anode and said cathode; and a control unit configured to control said motor to move said anode towards said cathode until a discharge current is detected by said current transducer control system, and when a discharge current is detected, to stop movement of the anode towards the cathode, and to determine a new movement speed and movement direction of said anode based on a consumption rate of said anode such that said discharge is interrupted. 2. The apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein said control unit is configured to move said anode away from said cathode. 3. The apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein said control unit is configured to stop movement of said anode, wait for a predetermined period of time, and to start movement of said anode towards said cathode. 4. The apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said cathode is arranged vertically in said container, extending substantially perpendicularly from a bottom of said container, and wherein said anode is arranged vertically in said container, above said cathode and facing said cathode. 5. The apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said cathode comprises graphite or tungsten. 6. The apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said anode consists of a combination of two materials. 7. The apparatus according to claim 6, wherein a composition of said anode is selected from the group comprising using Bi 2 Te 3 , Ag 3 Sn, Cu 6 Sn 5 , Cu 3 Sn, SiC, GaN, InP, SiGe, MoS 2 , WS 2 , BN, P, Fe, Ferrous alloys, stainless steel, Ti, Cu, Al, Sn, In and their alloys and WSe 2 . 8. The apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said liquid comprises a surfactant for preventing agglomeration of said nanoparticles. 9. The apparatus according to claim 8, wherein said surfactant is, Polyvinylpyrrolidone, PVP, or Polycarboxylate ether, PCE. 10. The apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said liquid comprises water soluble functionalized graphene configured to form a protective layer for said nanoparticles. 1 1 . The apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said anode and said cathode are cylindrical and arranged such that a base surface of said anode faces a base surface of said cathode. 12. The apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, comprising a plurality of anodes and corresponding cathodes arranged in parallel, each anode being arranged to face a corresponding cathode. 13. The apparatus according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein said nanoparticles have a size in the range of 1 nm to 100 nm. 14. A method for manufacturing nanoparticles in an apparatus comprising: a container; an anode and a cathode arranged facing each other in in said container, wherein said anode is movable in relation to said cathode such that a distance between said anode and said anode is controllable; a power supply connected to said anode and said cathode to form an electric field therebetween; a current transducer control system comprising a current sensor arranged to detect a discharge current from an electrostatic discharge between said anode and said cathode, wherein said discharge is configured to result in formation of metal nanoparticles through melting and evaporation of said anode, which nanoparticles are subsequently dispersed in said liquid; a motor configured to control said movement of said anode to control said distance between said anode and said cathode, said method comprising: providing a dielectric liquid in said container; providing a surfactant in said container; controlling said motor to move said anode towards said cathode; detecting a discharge current by said current transducer control system; stopping movement of the anode towards the cathode; and determining a new movement speed and movement direction of said anode based on a consumption rate of said anode such that said discharge is interrupted. 15. The method according to claim 14, further comprising moving said anode away from said cathode such that said discharge is interrupted. 16. The method according to claim 14, further comprising: waiting for a predetermined period of time; and moving said anode towards said cathode.
